heading: Reporting of tips
label: 6053 Reporting of tips

Effective Date of 1982 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–248, title III, § 314(e)96 Stat. 605Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 6001 and 6678 of this title, and enacting provisions set out as a note under this section] shall apply to calendar years beginning after .December 31, 1982“(2).—Special rule for 1983For purposes of section 6053(c) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954], in the case of payroll periods ending before , an employer must only report with respect to such periods—April 1, 1983“(A)amounts described in subparagraphs (A), (B), (C), and (D) of section 6053(c)(1) of such Code, and“(B)the name, and identification number, wages paid to, and tips reported by, each tipped employee.”

L. 89–97, title III, § 313(f)79 Stat. 385July 30, 1965“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section and amending sections 451, 3102, 3121, 3401, 3402, 6051, 6652, and 6674 of this title and , The Public Health and Welfare] shall apply only with respect to tips received by employees after 1965.”section 409 of Title 42

Regulations, , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title X, § 1072(b)98 Stat. 1052July 18, 1984“The Secretary of the Treasury shall prescribe by regulations within 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act [] the applicable recordkeeping requirements for tipped employees.”July 18, 1984

Threat of Audit Prohibited To Coerce Tip Reporting Alternative Commitment Agreements, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–206, title III, § 3414112 Stat. 755July 22, 1998“The Secretary of the Treasury or the Secretary’s delegate shall instruct employees of the Internal Revenue Service that they may not threaten to audit any taxpayer in an attempt to coerce the taxpayer into entering into a Tip Reporting Alternative Commitment Agreement.”

Modification of Tips Allocation Method, , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XV, § 1571100 Stat. 2765Oct. 22, 1986“Effective for any payroll period beginning after , an establishment may utilize the optional method of tips allocation described in the last sentence of section 31.6053–3(f)(1)(iv) of the Internal Revenue Regulations only if such establishment employs less than the equivalent of 25 full-time employees during such payroll period.”December 31, 1986

Study of Tip Compliance, , , directed Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate to submit before , to Committee on Ways and Means of House of Representatives and to Committee on Finance of Senate a report with respect to tip compliance in food and beverage service industry. Such study to include, but not be limited to, an analysis of tipping patterns, tip-sharing arrangements, and tip compliance patterns.Pub. L. 97–248, title III, § 314(c)96 Stat. 605Sept. 3, 1982Jan. 1, 1987

children.heading: Reports by employees
children.label: a Reports by employees
children.text: Every employee who, in the course of his employment by an employer, receives in any calendar month tips which are wages (as defined in section 3121(a) or section 3401(a)) or which are compensation (as defined in section 3231(e)) shall report all such tips in one or more written statements furnished to his employer on or before the 10th day following such month. Such statements shall be furnished by the employee under such regulations, at such other times before such 10th day, and in such form and manner, as may be prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Statements furnished by employers
children.label: b Statements furnished by employers
children.text: If the tax imposed by section 3101 or section 3201 (as the case may be) with respect to tips reported by an employee pursuant to subsection (a) exceeds the tax which can be collected by the employer pursuant to section 3102 or section 3202 (as the case may be), the employer shall furnish to the employee a written statement showing the amount of such excess. The statement required to be furnished pursuant to this subsection shall be furnished at such time, shall contain such other information, and shall be in such form as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe. When required by such regulations, a duplicate of any such statement shall be filed with the Secretary.

children.heading: Reporting requirements relating to certain large food or beverage establishments
children.label: c Reporting requirements relating to certain large food or beverage establishments
A
children.text: The gross receipts of such establishment from the provision of food and beverages (other than nonallocable receipts).

children.label: B
children.text: The aggregate amount of charge receipts (other than nonallocable receipts).

children.label: C
children.text: The aggregate amount of charged tips shown on such charge receipts.

children.label: i
children.text: the aggregate amount reported by employees to the employer under subsection (a), plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount the employer is required to report under section 6051 with respect to service charges of less than 10 percent.

children.label: E
children.text: With respect to each employee, the amount allocated to such employee under paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: The name and address of such employer.

children.label: B
children.text: The name of the employee.

children.label: C
children.text: The amount allocated to the employee under paragraph (3) for all payroll periods ending within the calendar year.

children.heading: Employee allocation of 8 percent of gross receipts
children.label: 3 Employee allocation of 8 percent of gross receipts
i
children.text: 8 percent of the gross receipts (other than nonallocable receipts) of such establishment for the payroll period, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount reported by such employees to the employer under subsection (a) for such period.

children.label: i
children.text: on the basis of a good faith agreement by the employer and the employees, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the absence of an agreement under clause (i), in the manner determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: The Secretary may lower the percentage required to be allocated
children.label: C The Secretary may lower the percentage required to be allocated
children.text: Upon the petition of the employer or the majority of employees of such employer, the Secretary may reduce (but not below 2 percent) the percentage of gross receipts required to be allocated under subparagraph (A) where he determines that the percentage of gross receipts constituting tips is less than 8 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: which provides food or beverages,

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to which the tipping of employees serving food or beverages by customers is customary, and

children.label: C
children.text: which normally employed more than 10 employees on a typical business day during the preceding calendar year.

children.heading: Employer not to be liable for wrong allocations
children.label: 5 Employer not to be liable for wrong allocations
children.text: The employer shall not be liable to any person if any amount is improperly allocated under paragraph (3)(B) if such allocation is done in accordance with the regulations prescribed under paragraph (3)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: carryout sales, or

children.label: B
children.text: services with respect to which a service charge of 10 percent or more is added.

children.heading: Application to new businesses
children.label: 7 Application to new businesses
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations for the application of this subsection to new businesses.

children.heading: Certified professional employer organizations
children.label: 8 Certified professional employer organizations
children.text: For purposes of any report required by this subsection, in the case of a certified professional employer organization that is treated under section 3511 as the employer of a work site employee, the customer with respect to whom a work site employee performs services shall be the employer for purposes of reporting under this section and the certified professional employer organization shall furnish to the customer and the Secretary any information the Secretary prescribes as necessary to complete such reporting no later than such time as the Secretary shall prescribe.
